title: ananassa sativa var. providentiae
intro: Ananassa sativa var. providentiae, also known as the guava, is a tropical evergreen shrub or small tree native to Central and South America. The medicinal properties of the guava plant are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of guava include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, as well as providing antioxidant protection against cell damage and oxidative stress. Guava is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is also consumed as a fresh fruit or used in jams and preserves.
